objective.subject: Mathematics
objective.statement: Read, plot and reason with coordinates in the first quadrant, describe translations left, right, up and down, and complete simple polygons from given points.
misconceptions: Reads the y-coordinate before the x-coordinate.
Counts grid lines inconsistently or starts counting from 1 instead of the origin.
Treats a translation as turning or resizing the shape.
Uses left and right correctly but reverses up and down on the vertical axis.
objective.parent_explanation: Can use grid coordinates to find and plot points, move them left, right, up or down, and complete simple shapes.
objective.teacher_evidence: Starts from the origin, uses x then y order, reads and plots points accurately, describes translations and reasons about missing vertices.

activities.title: I can plot points on a grid and describe moves using coordinates.
activities.prompt: Plot (4, 3) by counting four across and three up.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Find the origin, then trace across and up to the treasure point.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure the origin and the across-then-up coordinate convention.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Start at zero, zero. Count across on x, then count up on y.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A first-quadrant grid with a compass bot tracing an L-shaped route from the origin.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Plot (4, 3) by counting four across and three up.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce coordinates as ordered pairs that describe exact positions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The first number is across. The second number is up. Together they name one point.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The x-step and y-step light in different patterns before the point lands.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Read the coordinate of the flag point.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model reading a plotted point back into an ordered pair.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Drop a guide line to the x-axis, then across to the y-axis. Say x then y.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Guide lines project from a point to labelled axes.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Slide the gem from (2, 1) three squares right and two squares up.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect horizontal and vertical movements to changes in coordinate values.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Moving right increases x. Moving up increases y.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A gem token moves along arrow tracks while the coordinate updates.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which point is (2, 5), and which is (5, 2)?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: These two points use the same numbers, but the order changes the position.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two points pulse with swapped x and y labels.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rex plotted the last corner in the wrong place. Teach Rex how to complete the rectangle.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to reason from existing vertices to a missing polygon point.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I matched one number but forgot the other side of the rectangle. Can you show me the missing corner?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A rectangle ghost outline appears after the learner tests the x and y matches.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A pair of numbers that tells the position of a point on a grid.
vocabulary.audio_script: A coordinate gives the across number first and the up number second.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A glowing point labelled with an ordered pair, such as (3, 2).

vocabulary.child_explanation: The horizontal line where we count across.
vocabulary.audio_script: The x-axis runs left to right. Count across on x first.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: The bottom grid line lights from the origin to the target x-value.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The vertical line where we count up.
vocabulary.audio_script: The y-axis runs up and down. Count up after x.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: The side grid line lights upward from the origin to the target y-value.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A slide. The shape moves without turning or changing size.
vocabulary.audio_script: A translation slides every point the same distance in the same direction.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A shape slides along arrow tracks while staying the same size and orientation.

manipulative.purpose: Make first-quadrant coordinates visible through plotted treasure points, movement arrows and shape-completion challenges.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners trace from the origin, plot points, read coordinates, slide tokens by translations and place missing vertices to complete polygons.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Keyboard grid navigation, spoken coordinates, patterned axes, large touch targets, optional high-contrast grid, and reduced-motion static before-and-after panels.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm map colours, no flashing treasure effects and optional plain-grid mode.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace sliding animations with static start and end positions plus arrow labels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read coordinates as 'x equals four, y equals three' and as 'four across, three up'.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use persistent x-axis and y-axis labels, coordinate sentence frames and clear ordered-pair spacing.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Present one action at a time: find origin, count across, count up, place point.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow guide-line toggles, route replay, undo and confirm-before-submit.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame swapped coordinates as map clues and reward corrected tracing, not speed.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach coordinate, axis, origin, across, up, left, right, translate and vertex with icons.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify the origin and trace x then y without skipping zero.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: first-quadrant grid + highlighted route + origin or axis decision
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep grids small and axes clearly labelled for early items.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Plot given coordinates using across-then-up reasoning.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: ordered pair + empty first-quadrant grid + draggable point
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use swapped-coordinate distractors such as (2, 5) and (5, 2).

variant_blueprints.purpose: Read coordinates from plotted points using guide lines to both axes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: plotted point + multiple-choice ordered pairs + optional guide-line reveal
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include points on axes only after standard interior points are secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Describe and perform translations left, right, up and down on points and simple shapes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start coordinate or shape + movement instruction + end coordinate or route choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid moves that leave the first quadrant in review-stage sample items.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Complete rectangles and simple polygons by reasoning from known vertices.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: three vertices + target shape + missing vertex placement
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review needed for diagrams so the intended polygon is unambiguous.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads the y-coordinate before the x-coordinate.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses (5, 2) for a point at (2, 5), or plots swapped points.
repair_sequence: Trace across on the x-axis first.
Trace up on the y-axis second and say the ordered pair aloud.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner correctly plots or reads a swapped-pair item and explains 'across then up'.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts grid lines inconsistently or starts counting from 1 instead of the origin.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places points one square away from the intended coordinate.
repair_sequence: Label the origin as zero, zero.
Count jumps between grid lines, not labels as squares.
Replay the route with tick marks spoken aloud.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner places a point accurately on a new small grid without an off-by-one error.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats a translation as turning or resizing the shape.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner changes the shape's orientation or side lengths during a slide task.
repair_sequence: Show every vertex moving along equal parallel arrows.
Overlay the before and after shapes.
Ask what stayed the same and what changed.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner translates all vertices the same distance and states that the shape did not turn or resize.

feedback.companion_prompt: Rex plotted the last corner in the wrong place. Teach Rex how to complete the rectangle.
feedback.parent_summary: Can use grid coordinates to find and plot points, move them left, right, up or down, and complete simple shapes.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Starts from the origin, uses x then y order, reads and plots points accurately, describes translations and reasons about missing vertices.
next_actions: Teacher review of generated polygon diagrams before pilot.
Add larger first-quadrant grids after coordinate order is secure.
Interleave with area and perimeter grids once position language is stable.

hints: Count 4 across on x, then 3 up on y.
questions.explanation: (4, 3) means 4 across and 3 up from the origin.

body.prompt: A flag is plotted 2 squares across and 5 squares up. What is its coordinate?

hints: Coordinates are written x first, then y.
questions.explanation: The flag is at (2, 5) because it is 2 across on the x-axis and 5 up on the y-axis.

body.prompt: A gem starts at (2, 1). It moves 3 squares right and 2 squares up. Where does it finish?

hints: Moving right increases the first number.
Moving up increases the second number.
questions.explanation: From (2, 1), three right makes x = 5 and two up makes y = 3, so the gem finishes at (5, 3).

body.prompt: Which coordinate means 5 across and 2 up?
Three corners of a rectangle are (1, 1), (5, 1) and (1, 4). Where is the missing corner?

hints: The missing corner lines up with x = 5 and y = 4.
A rectangle has opposite sides that stay straight and parallel.
questions.explanation: The missing corner must share x = 5 with the right-hand corner and y = 4 with the top-left corner, so it is (5, 4).

reward_payload.explanation: Can use grid coordinates to find and plot points, move them left, right, up or down, and complete simple shapes.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Rex plotted the last corner in the wrong place. Teach Rex how to complete the rectangle.
